evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` is a high-fan-in hub (listed in packet as 244 importers) and mixes pure normalization (`reportError`) with UI side effects (`handleError` -> `notifyError`), so one entrypoint spans core + presentation concerns.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` both depend on this shared error hub, reinforcing broad coupling to a central shared module.

`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx` writes to `setProjectSelectionSnapshot(...)` in an effect, while `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` consumes and mutates optional global context state (`useShotAdditionSelectionOptional` + `clearSelectedShotForAddition()`), creating ambient cross-module coordination paths.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` composes both `baseButton` primitives and app-specific `buttonThemeVariants`, while neighboring `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `tooltip.tsx`, and `label.tsx` are primitive wrappers; this blurs what `shared/components/ui` is supposed to own.

`src/domains/generation/types/public.ts` is a high-fan-in barrel (listed in packet as 206 importers), and its centrality plus multiple shared hubs (`button.tsx`, `cn.ts`, `handleError.ts`) indicates top-level ownership is concentrated in broad entry modules rather than narrower capability seams.
`src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` defines app toaster runtime concerns under the same generic UI folder as primitive wrappers, further mixing infrastructure and primitive responsibilities.

`useImageLightboxRenderModel` and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` each rebuild near-identical `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, and `layoutProps` objects with media-specific branches (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:549`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:178`).

`useInlineEditState` assembles a very large `imageEditValue` state bag and then re-groups many of the same members again in the returned slices, indicating high ceremony/low leverage indirection (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:344`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:423`).

Task creation modules repeat large single-vs-batch param interfaces with mostly duplicated fields instead of composable contracts (`src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:24`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:51`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:110`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:127`).

`edit-images` depends directly on many internal `MediaLightbox` hooks/components/context modules, creating a feature-to-feature dependency seam instead of a stable shared editing contract (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:16`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:3`).

`DataFreshnessManager` (realtime/data layer utility) imports UI toast directly, coupling infrastructure logic to presentation concerns (`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts:11`, `src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts:188`).

`useSegmentSlotMode` also imports `MediaLightbox` slot-mode types from UI layer, extending UI coupling into travel editor state logic (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:12`).

Travel/join/segment task seams rely on manual key-by-key translation into orchestrator payload records with repeated fallback logic, making boundary behavior hard to reason about consistently (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:118`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:295`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:293`).

Each module chooses its own inclusion/override rules (conditional spreads, optional field injection, parser fallbacks), so handoff contracts are implicit and distributed rather than centralized (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:139`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:324`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:299`).

This same seam style appears on completion side where routing and payload-derived decisions are split across many helper layers, increasing choreography cost (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:103`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:209`).

Large option/state interfaces with many fields make call sites and updates noisy: `ImageLightboxProps` contains multiple grouped bags while still carrying many individual props (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:66`).

`UseSegmentSlotModeProps` is a wide parameter bag mixing navigation, persistence, frame updates, and structure-video operations in one hook boundary (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:24`).

`InlineEditStateResult` and related return-object assembly are very large, creating verbose plumbing and high local mutation surface (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:249`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:423`).

Generation/task lookup APIs expose incompatible failure shapes in adjacent modules: `useLineageChain` throws (`fetchVariantById`), `generationTaskRepository.getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns per-id status objects, and `generationTaskCache.preloadGenerationTaskMappings` consumes those statuses and only logs side effects.

`useTaskStatusCounts` exposes failure state in two parallel channels (`degraded`/`failedQueries`/`errorCode` plus nested `operation`), while other nearby service APIs (for example `generateVideo` and `consumePendingJoinClips`) use only `OperationResult`.

Edge helper APIs also mix conventions: `checkRateLimit` returns `ok: true` for fail-open degraded states, while `parseJsonBody` uses `ok: false` for malformed input, so callers cannot infer severity from `ok` consistently.

Multiple deprecated compatibility surfaces remain active: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts`, `compat/useCredits.ts`, `reportRuntimeError.ts`, `toolConstants.ts`, and `components/theme/buttonVariants.ts` all preserve old import paths with sunset comments and runtime warning hooks.

`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still accepts mixed canonical + legacy payload shapes (`starting_video_path`, `ending_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`, `portions_to_regenerate`) and translates them at runtime.

`legacyStructureVideo.ts` still publishes deprecated task fields (`structure_video_path`, `structure_video_motion_strength`, `use_uni3c`, `uni3c_*`) as part of exported interfaces, keeping old schema alive in type contracts.

`src/shared/hooks/useDarkMode.ts`, `useDebounce.ts`, and `useCredits.ts` are root-level compatibility re-exports while real implementations live in `src/shared/hooks/core/` or `src/shared/hooks/compat/`.

`src/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` and `AuthGate.tsx` are root-level re-export aliases to `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` and `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate.tsx`.
Directory profile shows `src/shared/components/` has 70 files with mixed zones (55 production, 15 test), indicating flattened ownership boundaries.

Directory profile shows `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` has 81 test files and imports `src/shared/hooks/` 79 times, while a separate root-level component test (`BulkEditControls.test.tsx`) remains outside mirrored test structure.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts` passes a wide prop bag into `useSubmissionHandlers`, which immediately repackages it again for `useSubmissionOrchestrator` with little behavioral transformation.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` reimplements frame-collision placement logic inline even though `src/shared/lib/timelinePositionCalculator.ts` already defines `ensureUniqueFrame`/placement helpers.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/index.ts` still centralizes request parsing, auth, rate-limiting, storage verification, task mutation, generation creation, orchestrator follow-up, and billing follow-up in a single request function.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` plus `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` rely on very wide composed prop/return interfaces, indicating boundary seams that are broad and costly to evolve.

findings.summary: Central error facade combines core error normalization with UI toast side effects.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both pure reporting (`reportError`, `reportAndRethrow`) and UI behavior (`handleError` -> `notifyError`) from one widely imported module.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` import from this hub directly, so feature hooks couple to the shared central module rather than a narrower boundary.
`supabase/client.ts` avoids `handleError` and uses `normalizeAndLogError` directly, showing both patterns coexist and boundary expectations are inconsistent.
findings.suggestion: Split the error API by layer: keep a pure core module for normalize/log/rethrow and move toast-enabled helpers to an explicit UI/runtime adapter module. Update callers so non-UI layers cannot import toast behavior by default.

findings.summary: Cross-module state coordination relies on ambient context/store channels instead of explicit boundaries.
evidence: `ProjectContext.tsx` mirrors selected project into external snapshot state via `setProjectSelectionSnapshot(...)` in `useEffect`, introducing a side channel outside provider consumers.

`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` reads optional global context (`useShotAdditionSelectionOptional`) and clears it inside an effect after calling `handleShotChange`, adding hidden inter-module behavior not visible in function args.
findings.suggestion: Replace ambient side channels with explicit boundary APIs: expose selection changes through dedicated hooks/events and keep one authoritative source per state domain (either context or store), then inject needed data into feature hooks through props or typed service interfaces.

findings.summary: `shared/components/ui` mixes primitives, app theming, and runtime behavior under one ownership bucket.
evidence: `button.tsx` merges `BASE_BUTTON_*` and `APP_BUTTON_*` variants, while `label.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`, and `cn.ts` are lightweight primitive wrappers.
`sonner.tsx` adds app toast runtime/provider behavior in the same folder as low-level primitives, so folder ownership is not domain-coherent.
findings.suggestion: Separate `shared/components/ui` into clear sub-boundaries (for example `ui/primitives`, `ui/theme`, `ui/runtime`) and move app-specific variant composition/toaster orchestration out of primitive-facing entry modules.

findings.summary: High-fan-in barrels/hubs concentrate ownership and make subsystem boundaries harder to reason about.
evidence: Packet marks these modules among top imported (`public.ts` 206, `button.tsx` 157, `cn.ts` 138, `handleError.ts` 244, `supabase/client.ts` 153), indicating many top-level dependencies flow through a small set of shared surfaces.
In reviewed files, these hubs expose broad utility surfaces reused across unrelated areas, which increases blast radius for design changes.
findings.suggestion: Introduce narrower capability-specific entrypoints (for example per-feature type exports and per-layer service adapters) and keep broad barrels only as thin compatibility facades during migration.

findings.summary: Image/video lightbox render models duplicate the same orchestration with minor media branches.
evidence: Both render-model builders reconstruct the same high-cardinality objects (`lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, `layoutProps`) and dependency arrays with mostly identical members.
Behavior drift risk is elevated because edits to one path require mirrored edits in the other; many keys are repeated verbatim except `isVideo`/URL selection and segment-slot specifics.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `useBaseLightboxRenderModel` that accepts a small media strategy object (video/image URL resolver, mode flags, slot-mode extras) and produces the common structures once. Keep media-specific logic in narrow adapters only.

findings.summary: Tool-specific editors depend on sibling feature internals and realtime logic depends on UI toast.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` imports a large set of `MediaLightbox` internals (hooks/context/utils), and `InlineEditView` imports `MediaLightbox` component internals directly.
`DataFreshnessManager` emits toast UI directly from a shared realtime manager class, binding infra behavior to UI availability and presentation decisions.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit shared boundary modules (e.g., `shared/editing-core` and `shared/realtime/notifications`) and depend on those contracts. Move UI toast emission behind injected notifier callbacks so realtime core is UI-agnostic.

findings.summary: Task handoffs rely on repeated ad-hoc payload mapping rules across task families.
evidence: Each task family manually maps many fields into `Record<string, unknown>` payloads with local precedence/fallback rules and conditional spread logic.
Completion/orchestrator logic then re-derives expected shape from these payloads, increasing translation points and coupling to implicit key conventions.
findings.suggestion: Define schema-backed task family contracts (typed read/write schemas) and generate mapper helpers from those schemas. Centralize precedence rules once per field family, then reuse in creator and completion paths.

findings.summary: Several hooks/components expose oversized state and options bags that obscure local intent.
evidence: `InlineEditStateResult` and `imageEditValue` assembly are large and repetitive, with many pass-through members grouped multiple times.
`ImageLightboxProps` and `UseSegmentSlotModeProps` combine many responsibilities into broad bag interfaces, increasing local branching and wiring overhead.
findings.suggestion: Split monolithic return/props bags into focused sub-models (`canvas`, `generationOps`, `navigation`, `structureVideoOps`) and pass only needed slices to each consumer. Prefer narrower selectors/hooks over one giant aggregate return shape.

findings.summary: Generation/task APIs use incompatible failure contracts across adjacent call paths
evidence: `useLineageChain.fetchVariantById` throws on scope/query failures.
`generationTaskRepository.getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns status-tagged mappings (`query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`, etc.) instead of throwing.
`generationTaskCache.preloadGenerationTaskMappings` writes cache entries and logs once for `query_failed`, but does not return mapping failure state to callers.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one boundary contract for generation/task lookups (prefer `OperationResult` with typed status payload), then adapt all three modules to that contract so callers can handle failures uniformly without hidden side effects.

findings.summary: Some APIs duplicate failure state in both top-level flags and OperationResult payloads
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` returns `degraded`, `failedQueries`, and optional `errorCode` while also embedding `operation` that encodes the same success/failure state.
`generateVideo` and `consumePendingJoinClips` expose only `OperationResult`, so consumers across these services must learn inconsistent response shapes for similar task-style workflows.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical envelope per service tier (for example `OperationResult` only, with typed success payload metadata), and remove duplicated top-level failure fields from `useTaskStatusCounts` or move all peers to the same dual-channel pattern.

findings.summary: `ok` semantics are inconsistent for degraded failures vs hard failures in edge helpers
evidence: `checkRateLimit` returns `operationSuccess` with `degraded` details when RPC/runtime failures happen (fail-open path).
`parseJsonBody` returns `operationFailure` for invalid body shape/JSON.
Both are `OperationResult`, but `ok` no longer reliably indicates healthy execution vs degraded fallback, forcing endpoint-specific interpretation.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared degraded/fallback contract in `edgeOperation` (for example explicit `state: 'success'|'degraded'|'failure'`) and migrate `checkRateLimit` and request parsing helpers to that contract so `ok` meaning is uniform.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion can trigger billing side effects even when status update no-ops
evidence: `markOrchestratorComplete` performs `.update(...).eq("id", orchestratorTaskId).in("status", ["Queued", "In Progress"])` and only checks `error`, not whether any row matched.
After that, it always calls `triggerCostCalculation` and `persistBillingOutcome`.
In concurrent completions, a second caller can miss the update precondition yet still execute external billing/reconciliation writes.
findings.suggestion: Make completion side effects conditional on a confirmed state transition (for example require `update(...).select('id').single()` or `count` check, and return early when 0 rows updated). Add an idempotency key/guard around `triggerCostCalculation` to prevent duplicate billing triggers.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias modules are still maintained across core surfaces
evidence: Each file exports deprecated compatibility APIs with removal targets and runtime warning hooks instead of being removed.
The aliases cover critical boundaries (Supabase client, credits hooks, error reporting, tool constants, UI variants), indicating migration is still broad rather than tail-end.
findings.suggestion: Run one migration sweep that rewrites remaining old import paths to canonical modules, then delete these alias modules together behind a single tracked cutoff release to prevent indefinite shim retention.

findings.summary: Task builders still encode legacy and canonical payload contracts simultaneously
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` keeps a large mixed-shape input contract and performs runtime translation/validation across old and new fields.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` exports deprecated legacy fields as part of active TypeScript interfaces, which keeps callers type-safe on old schema rather than forcing migration.
findings.suggestion: Narrow public task input types to canonical-only shapes and move legacy parsing to a short-lived ingestion boundary; once all callers are migrated, remove deprecated fields from exported interfaces to make old payloads compile-time failures.

findings.summary: Thumbnail-only preload mode still preloads full media URLs
evidence: In `preloader.ts`, `getImageUrl` uses `thumbnailOnly ? img.thumbUrl || img.url : img.url`, so when thumbnails are missing it silently falls back to full media.
This contradicts the `preloadThumbnailsOnly` contract and makes behavior depend on data completeness rather than explicit policy.
findings.suggestion: Make thumbnail-only mode strict: return `null` when `thumbUrl` is absent and let callers decide whether to schedule a separate full-media preload path.

findings.summary: Root-level compatibility aliases in shared hooks/components create ambiguous package ownership.
evidence: Each file is only a re-export shim, while actual code is in deeper domain folders (`core`, `compat`, `generation-controls`, `shared/auth/components`).
The alias files sit in top-level `shared` packages and present duplicate import paths for the same behavior.
Current imports already favor canonical paths for several modules, so aliases are largely migration residue rather than active boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Target folders: keep canonical implementations in `src/shared/hooks/core/`, `src/shared/hooks/compat/`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/`, and `src/shared/auth/components/`; create explicit legacy alias folders `src/shared/hooks/legacy-aliases/` and `src/shared/components/legacy-aliases/`. 2) Move order: first migrate all call sites off root aliases (`@/shared/hooks/useDarkMode`, `@/shared/hooks/useDebounce`, `@/shared/hooks/useCredits`, `@/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer`, `@/shared/components/AuthGate`) to canonical imports; then move alias implementations into legacy-alias folders; then leave thin temporary stubs at old paths for one release; then remove stubs once zero imports remain. 3) Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/use(DarkMode|Debounce|Credits)|@/shared/components/(AspectRatioVisualizer|AuthGate)" src`, apply path updates, then validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Top-level shared package roles are historical rather than domain-aligned, reducing architectural readability.
evidence: Files named as primary modules are pass-through layers, so file location and module responsibility diverge.
`src/shared/components/` contains both domain-specific aliases and colocated tests, while other areas use dedicated `__tests__` directories, creating inconsistent top-level decomposition.
This pattern repeats in both hooks and components, indicating a structural ownership-model drift, not a one-off file placement mistake.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Target folders: enforce domain-owned entrypoints (`src/shared/auth/components/`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/`, `src/shared/hooks/core/`) and centralize temporary compatibility in `src/shared/{hooks,components}/legacy-aliases/`; move component tests to `src/shared/components/__tests__/`. 2) Move order: first move `BulkEditControls.test.tsx` into `__tests__`; then convert root alias imports to canonical domain imports; then isolate/deprecate alias layer behind `legacy-aliases`; finally remove old root aliases after no-import verification. 3) Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "from './BulkEditControls'|@/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer|@/shared/components/AuthGate|@/shared/hooks/use(DarkMode|Debounce|Credits)" src/shared src/app`, update imports, then run `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Supabase client boundary depends on global init order instead of enforceable module contract
evidence: `client.ts` exposes `initializeSupabase()` and `getSupabaseClient()` with runtime throw on access-before-init.
`README.md` documents this as an app-startup rule, so correctness depends on cross-module sequencing discipline.
findings.suggestion: Replace order-coupled global access with an explicit provider/factory contract: (1) introduce a `SupabaseProvider` or `createSupabaseClient()` dependency passed from bootstrap, (2) keep a transitional facade that delegates to injected instance, (3) migrate callers to injected access, then (4) remove direct global getter and validate with `npm run typecheck && npm run test` plus a dedicated init-contract test.

findings.summary: Error facade mixes normalization API with toast side-effect policy in one public module
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports pure reporting (`reportError`) and UI-notifying behavior (`handleError` with `notifyError`) side by side.
`README.md` states notifier ownership is bootstrap-governed, but this module still exposes behavior that crosses core error and presentation concerns.
findings.suggestion: Split contract surfaces: keep `reportError/reportAndRethrow` in a core error module and move toast-presenting behavior to a UI adapter module (for example `errorHandling/presentation`), then update imports so non-UI code cannot reach toast behavior directly; validate with `npm run check:contracts && npm run test`.

findings.summary: Shared UI package is overloaded and mixes primitive, theme, hook, and runtime-contract concerns
evidence: Holistic structure shows `src/shared/components/ui/` has 60 files with mixed responsibilities and high inbound coupling.
`button.tsx` (theme-aware component contract) and `cn.ts` (cross-cutting utility contract) live in the same flat directory, increasing navigation and ownership ambiguity.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/components/ui/primitives`, `src/shared/components/ui/theme`, `src/shared/components/ui/runtime`, and `src/shared/lib/styling`; Stage 2 move in order `button.tsx`+`baseButton.tsx`+`buttonThemeVariants.ts` -> `ui/theme`, runtime registration helpers -> `ui/runtime`, and `cn.ts` -> `shared/lib/styling/cn.ts` with temporary re-export at old path; Stage 3 run import updates (`rg -l "@/shared/components/ui/cn" src | xargs sed -i '' 's#@/shared/components/ui/cn#@/shared/lib/styling/cn#g'`) and keep compatibility barrel exports; Stage 4 validate via `npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run quality:check`, then remove temporary re-exports.

findings.summary: Timeline duplicate-placement logic is duplicated instead of using shared calculator utilities.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` (around lines 427-450) manually performs expanding collision search (`+1/-1/+2/-2`) for duplicate target frames.
`timelinePositionCalculator.ts` already provides `ensureUniqueFrame` and placement helpers for exactly this uniqueness concern.
The hook comment says it uses the same logic as server-side mutation, but the logic is copied inline, creating drift risk when one side changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract duplicate-target computation into a shared `calculateDuplicateFrame(...)` helper built on `ensureUniqueFrame`, and call it from timeline orchestrator and any server/client mutation paths so placement rules are defined once.

findings.summary: Form submission flow uses low-leverage wrapper layers with repeated wide prop plumbing.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` destructures dozens of fields, writes `formStateRef`, builds `getTaskParams`, then forwards another large object to `useSubmissionHandlers`.
`useSubmissionHandlers` mainly re-forwards nearly the same fields to `useSubmissionOrchestrator` and then remaps command names.
`useSubmissionOrchestrator` contains repeated queue-task patterns (`submitManaged`, `queueExisting`, `queueLikeExisting`) with similar label/expectedCount/execute scaffolding.
findings.suggestion: Collapse this into a single domain hook (or a reducer-style submission service) with a typed `SubmissionContext` and reusable `queueSubmission(...)` primitive, so behavior branches differ only by policy inputs rather than repeated orchestration boilerplate.

findings.summary: Edge `complete_task` handler still mixes too many workflow responsibilities in one flow.
evidence: `completeTaskHandler` sequentially performs transport handling, auth, rate limit checks, storage checks, task-context fetch, param mutation, generation creation, task status update, orchestrator completion, and billing follow-up.
`orchestrator.ts` itself is another large control-flow hub, so orchestration complexity is split across two large procedural modules rather than explicit pipeline stages.
The composition boundary is centered on call order in one long function rather than a small set of cohesive lifecycle steps with narrow interfaces.
findings.suggestion: Refactor `complete_task` into explicit pipeline stages (request validation, authorization, storage resolution, task mutation, post-completion hooks) with stage contracts and a shared context object; keep orchestrator/billing hooks as pluggable post-processors.

findings.summary: Lightbox split introduced very wide interfaces that obscure ownership boundaries.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` imports many hooks and constructs multiple environment/shared/edit layers before render, indicating orchestration and view concerns remain intertwined.
`useSharedLightboxState` defines many grouped prop sub-interfaces and a large nested return contract, effectively functioning as a broad parameter bag API.
The breadth of inputs/outputs increases coupling cost for each feature addition because many call sites and type groupings must be updated together.
findings.suggestion: Split shared lightbox state into narrower domain slices (variants, shot-actions, references/join, navigation) behind composable hooks with minimal contracts, and have `ImageLightbox` assemble only the slices required for active feature flags.
